title: quercus lusitanica var. boissieri
intro: Quercus lusitanica var. boissieri, also known as Portuguese oak, is a variety of the holm oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high tannin content, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus lusitanica var. boissieri include its potential to reduce inflammation, lower cholesterol levels, and act as an antioxidant. Quercus lusitanica var. boissieri is often used to prepare herbal infusions, teas, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
